description: SAM.gov Master Extract data element mapping with field definitions, datatypes, enumerations, and sensitivity levels

data_elements.definition:
The UNIQUE ENTITY ID for the entity.
It is 12-character alpha-numeric. Example: F847A1795DE4
Blank Spaces, formerly occupied by the prior Entity identifier

data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Mandatory to enter EFT INDICATOR if the Entity has multiple bank accounts.
Mandatory if DoD entity

data_elements.definition: Single character code that defines the status of the record being sent in the extract. The monthly file contains the complete record whether active or expired. For the daily files, the SAM Extract Code further specifies which data elements are sent for the record.
data_elements.enumeration:
Monthly File:
A - Active - Send the Complete Record.
E - Expired - Send the Complete Record.

Daily File:
1 - Deleted/Deactivated Record - Send UEI(SAM), EFT INDICATOR, CAGE Code, DODAAC, SAM Extract Code, Purpose of Registration
2 - New Active Record - Send the Complete Record.
3 - Updated Active Record - Send the Complete Record.
4 - Expired Record - Send UEI(SAM), EFT INDICATOR, CAGE Code, DODAAC, SAM Extract Code, Purpose of Registration

Z1 - Federal Assistance Awards
Z2 - All Awards
Z3 - IGT-Only
Z4 - Federal Assistance Awards & IGT
Z5 - All Awards & IGT

data_elements.definition: The date the initial entity registration was activated either in SAM or its predecessor, CCR. This date does not change.
The date this current registration will expire or has already expired. This date is 365 days after the user submitted the registration.

The date this registration was last updated.  This can change while the Activation Date remains the same for many reasons, such as POC changes, reference data changes, or SAM Technical Team database changes.
The date this registration record was made active.  This can differ from the date that the registration was submitted due to the time it takes for TIN Validation and CAGE Code Assignment/Validation.
This is part of the entity's Physical Address.  See SAM Functional Data Dictionary for more information.
This is part of the entity's Physical Address.  See SAM Functional Data Dictionary for more information.
This is part of the entity's Physical Address.  See SAM Functional Data Dictionary for more information.
This is part of the entity's Physical Address.  See SAM Functional Data Dictionary for more information.

data_elements.definition: This flag indicates whether the Business Name and Physical Address sourced from D&B.

data_elements.enumeration:
90 -  Government Non-Classified
92 -  Government Confidential
93 -  Government Secret
94 -  Government Top Secret

90 -  Government Non-Classified
92 -  Government Confidential
93 -  Government Secret
94 -  Government Top Secret

2J  - Sole Proprietorship 
2K  - Partnership or Limited Liability Partnership
2L  - Corporate Entity (Not Tax Exempt) 
8H  - Corporate Entity (Tax Exempt) 
2A  - U.S. Government Entity 
CY  - Country - Foreign Government 
X6  - International Organization 
ZZ - Other

data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Mandatory if "Country of Incorporation" is USA

data_elements.definition: Four digit identifier that provides the number of business types to be provided in the business type String.
Tilde (~) delimited string that includes all of the entity's business type information.  For more information on the business types, See the STRING Clarification TAB
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: If Counter is greater than "0000" string is mandatory

data_elements.definition: This data element identifies what business purpose that the IGT registration (Purpose of Registration Z3, Z4, Z5) has registered for.  The potential values represent Buyer, Seller, Buyer & Seller.
Four digit identifier that provides the number of NAICS Codes to be provided in the NAICS Code String.
Tilde (~) delimited string that includes all of the entity's NAICS information.  For more information on the NAICS Code String, See the STRING Clarification TAB
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: If Counter is greater than "0000" string is mandatory

data_elements.definition: Four digit identifier that provides the number of PSC Codes to be provided in the PSC Code String.
Tilde (~) delimited string that includes all of the entity's PSC information.  For more information on the PSC Code String, See the STRING Clarification TAB
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: If Counter is greater than "0000" string is mandatory

data_elements.enumeration:
M - US Mail
F - FAX
E - Email

data_elements.definition: Four digit identifier that provides the number of NAICS Exceptions to be provided in the NAICS Exception String.

Tilde (~) delimited string that includes exception information for any NAICS Codes that the user has selected that has exceptions.  For more information on the NAICS Exception String, See the STRING Clarification TAB
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: If Counter is greater than "0000" string is mandatory

data_elements.enumeration:
Y - The entity has delinquent debt subject to offset.
N - The entity does not have delinquent debt subject to offset.
Null - SAM has not received information from the Treasury Offset Program (TOP) for this entity, or, this entity does not fall under the TOP.
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Only applies to entities which provide a Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) during registration.

data_elements.definition: This flag indicates whether the entity has an active exclusion record. If this flag is set to “D”, this will indicate that the registrant has an exclusion record.  A null value indicates that this entity does not have an active exclusion record.
Four digit identifier that provides the number of SBA Business Types to be provided in the SBA Business Types String.

Tilde (~) delimited string that includes SBA business type  and expiration information as provided to SAM by SBA.  For more information on the SBA Business Types String, See the STRING Clarification TAB
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: If Counter is greater than "0000" string is mandatory

data_elements.definition: Four digit identifier that provides the number of Numeric Types to be provided in the SAM Numerics Code String.

Tilde (~) and caret (^) delimited string that includes Numerics data as collected in the assertions page of the SAM Registration. For more information on the SAM Numerics Code String, See the STRING Clarification TAB
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: If Counter is greater than "0000" string is mandatory

data_elements.enumeration:
NPDY - User did not authorize their public registration data to be included in SAM Public Search results. 
Null Value - User authorized their public registration data to be included in SAM Public Search results.
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Mandatory to be present if the entity has opted out of public search.

data_elements.definition: Four digit identifier that provides the number of Disaster Response Types to be provided in the Disaster Response String.

Tilde (~) and caret (^) delimited string that includes Disaster Response data as collected in the assertions page of the SAM Registration. For more information on the Disaster Response String, See the STRING Clarification TAB
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: If Counter is greater than "0000" string is mandatory

data_elements.definition: Annual IGT Revenue - This data element details the amount of revenue the Intra-Governmental Transfer (IGT) entity receives via IGT transactions in a given year.
This data element identifies a Federal Government Entity as  either a 'DoD' entity or a 'Federal' Entity
data_elements.enumeration:
DoD - Entity exists within DoD
Federal - Entity exists within a Department that is not DoD
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Mandatory for U.S Federal / DoD entities

data_elements.definition: The two-digit Agency/Department code derived from the Treasury Index.
The four digit code that identifies the Department ID for the Federal Government Entity.  The Department codes were originally sourced from FPDS and the NIST/FIPS 800-87 Document.
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Mandatory if Entity is U.S.Federal Government

data_elements.definition: The functional name that corresponds with the Department Code.
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Mandatory if Entity is U.S.Federal Government

data_elements.definition: The four digit code that identifies the Agency ID for the Federal Government Entity.  The Agency codes were originally sourced from FPDS and the NIST/FIPS 800-87 Document.
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Mandatory if Entity is U.S.Federal Government

data_elements.definition: The functional name that corresponds with the Agency Code.
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Mandatory if Entity is U.S.Federal Government

data_elements.definition: The office code is an up-to six character identifier representing a U.S. government office in the SAM Federal hierarchy. This code exists independently of the unique entity identifier used for registration purposes, i.e. UEI(SAM), DoDAAC, or CAGE Code.
data_elements.substitution_for_mandatory: Mandatory if Entity is U.S.Federal Government
